questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Column-I describes various arrangements to obtain interference pattern on screen.
questions.solution:
Path difference remains same on a circle for case D
Shape of fringe pattern for pin hole is hyperbolic
Shape of fringe pattern for slit is straight line
$\Delta \mathrm{x}_{\max }$ can't be greater than ' d ' distance between the source in $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B} \& \mathrm{C} \& \Delta \mathrm{x}_{\min }$ can't be less than d-distance between the source in D .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two coherent sources $S_1$ and $S_2$ are kept on the edges of a step as shown in the figure. An infinitely long screen is placed on the right side of sources and lies along $y-z$ plane. Calculate total number of maximas (excluding maxima at infinity) observed on the screen. $\mathrm{PS}_1=8 \lambda$ and $\mathrm{PS}_2=6 \lambda$. Where $\lambda$ is wavelength of light used. (There is no reflection from steps)
questions.solution:
Path difference varies from to $+10 \lambda$ to $-8 \lambda$.
Total no. of maxima $=10+8$ (excluding the one at $-8 \lambda$ as this will never be achieved)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: At a point on the screen in YDSE experiment 3rd maxima is observed at $t=0$. Now screen is slowly moved with constant speed away from the slits in such a way that the centre of slits and centre of screen lie on the same, line always and at $t=1 \mathrm{sec}$ the intensity at that point is observed (3/4)th of maximum intensity in between 2nd and 3rd maxima. The speed of screen may be ($D=$ separation between the screen and slits $d=$ separation between the slits, $d$ « $D, \lambda=5000 Å$)
questions.solution:
$O P=\frac{3 \lambda D}{d}$
Corresponding path difference at position mentioned is $(2+1 / 6) \lambda$ or $(3-1 / 6) \lambda$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{13}{6} \frac{\lambda(D+v)}{d}=\frac{3 \lambda D}{d} \Rightarrow v=\frac{5 D}{13} \\ & \text { or } \frac{17 \lambda}{6 d}(D+v)=\frac{3 \lambda D}{d} \Rightarrow v=\frac{D}{17}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $A B C$ is a spherical wave front centred at $O$ symmetric about $B E$ is incident on silts $S_1$ and $S_2 . B S_1=3 \lambda, S_1 S_2$ $=4 \lambda, B O=6 \lambda, S_1 E=128 \lambda$ and $\lambda$ is the wavelength of incident wave. A mica sheet of refractive index 1.5 is pasted on $S_2$. The minimum value of thickness of mica sheet for which central bright fringe forms at $E$ will be
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & O S_1=3 \lambda \\ & O S_2=5 \lambda\end{aligned}$
$S_2$ leads $S_1$ by $2 \lambda$ Net phase at $E$ must be zero $\begin{aligned} & -2 \lambda+\sqrt{(128 \lambda)^2+(4 \lambda)^2}-128 \lambda+t / 2=0 \\ & t / 2=\frac{31 \lambda}{16} \Rightarrow t=\frac{31 \lambda}{8} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A parallel beam of visible light consisting of wavelengths $\lambda_1$ and $\lambda_2$ is incident on a standard YDSE apparatus with $\mathrm{d}=1 \mathrm{~mm}, \mathrm{D}=1 \mathrm{~m} . \mathrm{P}$ is a point on the screen at a distance $y$ from center of screen $O$. At $y=y_1$ is the nearest point above $O$ where the two maxima coincide and at $y=y_2$ is the nearest point above $O$ where the two minima coincide. $\beta_1 \& \beta_2$ are fringe width corresponding to wave length $\lambda_1$ and $\lambda_2$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & y_1=n_1 \beta_1=n_2 \beta_2=L C M \text { of } \beta_1 \text { and } \beta_2 \\ & 2 y_1=2 n_1 \beta_1=2 n_2 \beta_2\end{aligned}$
Hence at this point both maxima again coincide
$\mathrm{y}_2\left(\mathrm{n}_1-\frac{1}{2}\right) \beta_1=\left(\mathrm{n}_2-\frac{1}{2}\right) \beta_2 ; \frac{\beta_1}{\beta_2}=\frac{\mathrm{n}_2-\frac{1}{2}}{\mathrm{n}_1-\frac{1}{2}} \Rightarrow \frac{\beta_1}{\beta_2}=\frac{2 \mathrm{n}_1-1}{2 \mathrm{n}_1-1}$
Which will have a solution. If $\frac{\beta_1}{\beta_2}$ expressed as a proper fraction will be of form $\frac{\text { odd }}{\text { odd }}$.

For (B and C) : $\frac{\beta_1}{\beta_2}$ is of form $\frac{\text { Odd }}{\text { even }}$. Hence no solution i.e. the two minima will never coincide.

For (A) $\frac{\beta_1}{\beta_2}$ is of form $\frac{\text { odd }}{\text { odd }}$. Hence at some finite $y_2$ the two minima will coincide.
At $2 y_2$ the two maxima (and not minima) will coincide.
$\therefore \mathrm{y}=3 \mathrm{y}_2$ is the next nearest point where minima coincide.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
